title: CBS News Bodycam Footage Contradicts ICE Account in Texas Killing of Ruben Martinez
excerpt: Bodycam footage obtained by CBS News appeared to show that ICE agents had positioned themselves in front of Ruben Ray Martinez's slowly moving car before opening fire—directly contradicting DHS's claim that Martinez 'accelerated' and 'intentionally' ran over an agent. Martinez was shot three times and then handcuffed on the ground before receiving medical attention.
content:
On March 7, 2026, CBS News published police bodycam footage from the March 2025 fatal shooting of U.S. citizen Ruben Ray Martinez by an ICE HSI agent in South Padre Island, Texas. The footage raised serious questions about the official account.

  • The footage appeared to show federal agents standing in front of Martinez's car as it slowly moved forward, contradicting ICE's claim that Martinez 'accelerated' and 'intentionally ran over' an agent.
  • Martinez's attorneys stated: 'These new videos confirm that Ruben's car was barely moving when he was shot. That he was braking, not accelerating. That nobody was on the hood of his car. That nobody was in front of his car when he was shot.'
  • After being shot three times, Martinez was pulled from his vehicle, thrown face-down to the ground, and handcuffed by an ICE agent. Personnel on the scene did not provide medical care until after he was handcuffed—approximately two minutes after the shooting.
  • DHS policy advises agents to 'avoid intentionally and unreasonably placing themselves in positions in which they have no alternative to using deadly force'—a provision potentially violated if agents positioned themselves in the vehicle's path.
  • Acting ICE Director Todd Lyons responded: 'We stand by the grand jury's unanimous decision that found no criminality.'
mediaPlaceholder: South Padre Island police bodycam video showing ICE agents' positioning relative to Martinez's car before shooting
